title: tpm — faaas_kiwi_project_pptx — v1
description: Synthesized by skill_builder for persona=tpm, kb=faaas_kiwi_project_pptx. Refine before promoting.

week_id.description: Set the reporting week identifier for this deck (e.g., "2026-W20" or "Week of 2026-05-18"). If the Confluence page explicitly states a week/date range, extract it; otherwise derive from the page last-updated date and label as "Week of <YYYY-MM-DD>".

program_name.description: Extract the program name exactly as titled on the Confluence status hub page (use the page heading/title).

program_purpose.description: Extract the 1-sentence purpose/goal statement from the Confluence page (why the program exists). Keep it exec-safe and concise.

orm_status.description: Extract the ORM (operational risk management) status if explicitly stated on the Confluence page (e.g., "Open", "In review", "Closed", plus any ID). If not present, return "Not stated in source"—do not infer.

rag_summary.description: Derive a single exec-ready RAG line by summarising the overall program status across the Confluence workstreams (security scanning/remediation, fleet patching, DB patching, OS patching). Must include (1) Red/Amber/Green and (2) a 1-sentence justification grounded in the page’s stated progress metrics, capacity constraints, and decision dependencies.

schedule_health.description: Derive schedule health as a concise statement (e.g., "On track", "At risk", "Slipping") by combining Confluence evidence about timing (e.g., proposal timing, meeting dates) and constraints (e.g., limited monthly patching slots, pending leadership decisions). State: "Schedule is <status> because <reason>; mitigation is <action>".

key_metrics.description: Extract and normalise the key numeric KPIs from the Confluence page into a short list. Include security scan completion (%), scanned/total repos, remaining repos, and DB patching completion counts (prod/non-prod/demo) when present. Do not add metrics not stated in the source.

blocking_issues.description: Derive blockers by aggregating items explicitly labelled as asks/decisions needed/capacity gaps/dependencies in the Confluence page (e.g., leadership clarity needed, capacity shortfalls, ERs required). For each blocker, include a one-line description, owner (team/person if named), and any stated target date; if no date is stated, use "Not stated". Return an empty array if none are supported by the page.

next_steps.description: Derive next steps by summarising the Confluence action-oriented statements across workstreams (e.g., working group scheduling, accelerate triage, proposal review, file ERs). Each item must be time-bound using a stated date from the page; if no date is stated, use "this week" (weekly cadence) and keep the action concrete.

exec_asks.description: Derive exec asks by aggregating explicit leadership asks/decisions requested in the Confluence page (e.g., monthly full fleet patching proposal, whether monthly DB patching is required). Each ask must be phrased as a decision or approval request and include the consequence if delayed, based only on source text.

workstream_status_snapshot.description: Derive a program-level snapshot by summarising each Confluence workstream (#1–#4) into: workstream name, current status (1 sentence), and the primary risk stated or implied by that workstream’s section. Use exec-safe language and avoid dumping sub-bullets.

leadership_decisions_needed.description: Derive a consolidated list of leadership decisions needed by combining Confluence statements that indicate a decision point (e.g., "need clarity from leadership", "options under review"). Each item should include decision statement and the impacted area (fleet patching, DB patching, etc.).

dependencies_and_ers.description: Extract dependencies and ERs by aggregating the Confluence-listed dependency items (e.g., ERs such as bulk cancel API, customer notification blurb). For each, capture a short description and any owner/team if stated; do not invent IDs.

links_index.description: Extract a curated list of operational links from the Confluence page (Jira dashboard, Jira filter, security dashboard, repo list, spreadsheet, code repository link). Provide each as {label, url}. Exclude internal chatter links unless they are the only contact path.

owners_and_contacts.description: Extract named owners/participants and their associated area from the Confluence page (e.g., working group participants, patching owners under review, named leads). Return as a concise list; do not infer roles beyond what is stated.
